summary:
Washing an elastic impression with a slurry helps prevent syneresis, which maintains its shape and reduces inaccurate results after setting. It ensures better control over the material properties crucial for precise dental work. To summarize concisely while capturing essential details,

To ensure accurate elasticity post-setting, washes remove excess fluids; thus, avoiding syneresis preserves the impression's form and enhances final restoration outcomes. Sentence two captures core aspects without extraneous information efficiently. Agent
Certainly! 

Before pouring an elastic impression, washing it with a slurry prevents syneresis, maintaining its shape and reducing inaccuracies. Proper handling controls moisture content, ensuring precise restorations.
